structure.notes: The required Stage 0 roles and PartialID locked-parameter block are mostly present, and the section-7 bounding-functional usage is cluster-appropriate. However, the note omits the in-repository prior-work positioning present in the Stage -1 proposal, and the formalization checklist does not explicitly separate the law-invariant probabilistic assumptions from the deterministic LP/inequality objects needed to police the density-version issue.

novelty.notes: The actual derivation is at most subfield/incremental relative to the novelty_target=field floor. Once pointwise sharp intervals and absence of cross-dose compatibility restrictions are assumed, the main equality is essentially product/coupling plus intersection with a finite monotone-concave cone; the shape tightening is a standard LP projection phenomenon. The note does not establish a genuinely new continuous-dose sensitivity regime beyond the locked grid-version construction.

correctness.notes: The main pasting proof relies on assigning continuous-treatment density values at finitely many points and using those version-dependent values as substantive restrictions. This must be made mathematically explicit as a version-level model primitive, or the theorem is not a law-invariant partial-identification statement. Proposition 2's proof also asserts feasibility of a displayed vector that need not be monotone when L1>L2, although the formula may be repairable by a different feasible completion.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:sharp-grid} / Lemma \ref{lem:pasting}
perItemFindings.one_line: The reverse-inclusion proof changes or prescribes density values at finitely many continuous-dose points; without treating density versions as part of the model, the restriction is not a property of the full-data law.

perItemFindings.label: Novelty tier
perItemFindings.one_line: Assessed tier is subfield or incremental, below novelty_target=field; the kernel is generic finite-grid shape closure of assumed sharp pointwise intervals, not a field-level new bound or regime.

perItemFindings.label: Related work and positioning
perItemFindings.one_line: The note omits the Stage -1 in-repository comparison and does not sufficiently distinguish the theorem from standard Manski-style completion plus shape-restricted LP projection.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition \ref{prop:three-dose}
perItemFindings.one_line: The proof claims the vector (L0,max{L1,c},L2) is feasible under the stated condition, but this vector can violate monotonicity when L1>L2.

perItemFindings.label: Assumption \ref{ass:pointwise}
perItemFindings.one_line: A major part of the sharpness burden is shifted into a labelled pointwise-sharpness assumption, leaving the proved contribution mainly as a coupling/pasting lemma under independent grid-dose restrictions.

verbatim_critique:
The note is structurally close to the required Stage 0 format for a PartialID anchor: it has the locked tuple (pid_dose_response_shape, v1, P, theta, R_{Gamma,mc}, B_{Gamma,mc}), labelled assumptions, a cluster-appropriate bounding functional in the fixed 'Regression estimator' section, a target-estimand section that does not define the target tautologically, main results, proofs, sanity checks, a minimality check, and a math-object checklist. The structural defects are secondary: the related-work section drops the in-repository comparison that was present in the proposal, and the formalization checklist should more explicitly expose the density-version/model-primitive issue as a probabilistic object rather than treating it as an ordinary deterministic pasting identity.

The main correctness concern is Lemma \ref{lem:pasting}. The construction sets f_{Q_v}(a_j | X,U)=e(a_j | X)q_j(U,X) at finitely many design doses and then says that changing density values on a finite set does not change the integrated conditional law of A. That observation is true, but it is also the problem: for a continuous treatment, density values at individual points are version-dependent and are not law-invariant features of Q. Thus the displayed complete-over-nominal ratio at A=a_j is being controlled by choosing a density version on a null set, not by a substantive restriction on the conditional treatment law, unless the locked model explicitly makes density versions part of the primitive admissible structure. The theorem can be correct only in that version-level/grid-specialized sense. As a journal-style partial-identification statement about laws, this is a serious gap because the asserted sharpness may be an artifact of modifiable density representatives.

The proof of Proposition \ref{prop:three-dose} has a local algebraic/feasibility gap. It lower-bounds mu_1 by max{L1,c(L0,L2)}, but then asserts that the vector (L0,max{L1,c},L2) is feasible under the proposition's upper-endpoint condition. If L1>L2, that displayed vector violates monotonicity even when the full polytope is nonempty. The proposition may be repairable by choosing the third coordinate as max{L2,m} with m=max{L1,c}, subject to the existing nonemptiness/upper feasibility implications, but the current proof is not correct as written.

The novelty claim does not clear the orchestrator's field-tier floor. The derivation assumes pointwise sharp no-shape intervals in Assumption \ref{ass:pointwise}; assumes no cross-dose smoothness or compatibility restrictions in the locked grid-specialized CMSM; then proves that independently realizable coordinates can be coupled and intersected with monotone-concave inequalities. That is useful as a finite-grid technical lemma, but it is not a field-level new sharp bound in the sense of resolving a previously open substantive continuous-dose sensitivity regime. The three-dose phase transition is a standard concavity chord inequality applied to interval endpoints. The note also does not adequately compare this kernel to ordinary Manski completion arguments, shape-restricted LP projection, or the in-repository PartialID artifacts cited in the Stage -1 proposal. Because the assessed tier_at_derivation is below field and the gap appears structural to the locked grid-version formulation, the artifact should not be accepted under novelty_target=field.
